_meta.purpose: Reviewed calibration corpus of knowledge that MUST survive the importance classifier. Every entry here is knowledge a human reviewer confirmed is worth keeping. The deterministic rules may never hard-veto any of them: a veto here means the rule is too aggressive and the RULE must be fixed, never the fixture.
_meta.content: All examples are synthetic or anonymized. No real credentials, hostnames, customer names, or private repository content.
_meta.adversarial_block: Fixtures with an 'adv-' id prefix are adversarial: they deliberately use vocabulary the veto rules key on (exit codes, tool calls, test outcomes, very short normative rules) so a green run is evidence the rules generalize, not that they were co-fitted to the rest of the corpus. Added after review found the agent_operation_only and empty_content vetoes false-vetoed real knowledge that happened to mention this vocabulary.
_meta.veto_probes: Every entry under `fixtures` carries at least one positive knowledge signal, and the agent_operation_only veto only fires when NO knowledge assertion is present — so no fixture in `fixtures` can ever exercise that veto. `veto_probes` exists to close that blind spot and pins the rule from BOTH directions. `veto_probes.must_not_be_vetoed` is real, durable knowledge that carries NO positive knowledge-assertion marker and yet uses the exact vocabulary the veto keys on ('task completed', 'let us', 'a executar', 'here is a summary', 'running the tests', 'reading the file', 'all tests pass'); it must never be vetoed AND must trigger no positive signal, so a green run proves the ANCHORING is right rather than that a positive phrase was bolted on. `veto_probes.must_be_vetoed` is unambiguous agent chatter that must be vetoed by agent_operation_only. If an entry under must_not_be_vetoed fails, the RULE is wrong, never the fixture.
_meta.v3_reach: v3 deleted the gerund-subject narration branch and the progress-tail machinery: a sentence whose subject is a gerund over the agent's own tooling ('Running the tests now, 3 failed.', 'Reading the file config/rag.php…') is inherently ambiguous with a statement about the system ('Running the tests in parallel corrupts the sqlite database.'), and three rounds of tail-tuning failed to separate them. Those gerund progress reports were therefore REMOVED from must_be_vetoed: they now escape to the semantic judge, which scores them low. That is a deliberate, accepted recall loss — a missed veto costs one judge call, a false veto destroys knowledge forever.
_meta.v4_reach: v4 closed the last two structural holes, WITHOUT adding a single word to any marker list. (1) TAIL DISCIPLINE: shape (a) — a first-person operational clause over a tooling artefact — now has the same whole-sentence discipline shape (b) always had. A shape-(a) sentence that opens a FURTHER FINITE CLAUSE (',' ';' ':' '—', a subordinator/relativiser, or a coordinator that is not coordinating another bare infinitive) is ambiguous and escapes: 'I checked the logs and the worker restarts on every deploy.' is a fact, and v3 destroyed it. (2) HEAD-NOUN ANCHORING: the tooling keyword must now be the HEAD of the object, not merely its first noun, so 'the file naming convention', 'the test database credentials', 'the log format' and 'the diff algorithm' are no longer tooling artefacts and escape. TRUE REACH OF THE VETO AFTER v4: a sentence is narration only when it is (a) a SENTENCE-INITIAL first-person operational clause over a tooling artefact with NO further finite clause ('I ran the tests.', "I'm checking the file now.", 'Let me open the file.', 'Corri os testes.'), or (b) a terse status phrase that is the WHOLE sentence ('All tests pass.', 'Done.'), and EVERY sentence of the candidate is one of those, and the candidate asserts no knowledge anywhere. CHATTER MOVED OUT OF must_be_vetoed IN v4: 'chatter-i-ran-the-tests' ('I ran the tests and all tests pass now, the tool call returned successfully.') now ESCAPES, because the coordinated clause after 'and' is a further finite clause and the rule can no longer tell it apart from 'I ran the tests and the seeder populates the demo tenant.' — which is knowledge. That is a deliberate, accepted recall loss. The bare form ('I ran the tests.') is still vetoed and is pinned by 'chatter-i-ran-the-tests-bare'.
_meta.v5_reach: v5 INVERTS the shape-(a) predicate. Rounds 1-4 all defined agent narration by enumerating what BREAKS it: a marker list, then a progress-tail reader, then a class of clause-opening separators (',' ';' ':' en-dash, em-dash) plus a head-noun anchor. Every round, an adversarial reviewer found real facts hard-vetoed through the separator that had NOT been enumerated -- '(', '[', a quote, the ASCII '--', '/', a newline -- and the head-noun anchor additionally failed whenever the true head noun ended the sentence ('I updated the log format.'). A blacklist of the ways a sentence can carry a fact cannot be completed; the next round always finds one more. The blacklist WAS the bug. In v5, shape (a) is a WHITELIST carrying exactly the whole-sentence discipline shape (b) always had: ^ <first-person operational opener> <tooling-artefact object> <terminal punctuation only> $. NOTHING may follow the object -- no parenthetical, bracket, quote, dash, slash, newline, coordinated clause, preposition, second noun, code path or adverb. There is no separator class to under-list, because nothing is listed: the sentence must simply be OVER. ACCEPTED, DELIBERATE RECALL LOSS: the veto now reaches only BARE operational clauses ('I ran the tests.', "I'm checking the file.", 'Let me open the file.', 'Corri os testes.'), terse status phrases ('All tests pass.', 'Done.'), and content made entirely of those. Chatter with ANY tail ('I ran the tests and all tests pass now.', 'I read the diff (nothing changed).', 'I have updated the file app/Services/Foo.php with the new signature.') escapes to the semantic judge, which scores it low. A missed veto costs one judge call; a false veto destroys knowledge forever. must_be_vetoed has therefore been PRUNED to only what the rule still reaches: the corpus states the truth about the rule, it does not assert a reach the rule does not have.
_meta.v6_reach: v6 closes the TITLE channel — the same failure class rounds 1-5 removed from the content channel, surviving in the title. The v5 narration predicate read `content` ALONE, while the hasKnowledgeAssertion() safety net read title+content. So when the durable fact lived in the TITLE and the content was bare narration (title: 'The worker restarts on every deploy' / content: 'I checked the logs.'), the GRAMMAR never saw the fact, and the hand-tuned GENERAL_ASSERTION_MARKERS lexicon was the only thing standing between real knowledge and a hard veto. It saved 'The parser rejects every uppercase key' (because 'rejects' happens to be listed) and destroyed 'The parser lowercases every key' (because 'lowercases' is not): same fact, different verb, opposite outcome — precision decided by a word list. THE FIX applies the round-5 whitelist principle to the title. A fourth condition requires the TITLE to be NON-INFORMATIVE: empty, a generic placeholder ('Note', 'Nota', 'Untitled', 'Sem título'), or itself agent narration / a terse status phrase under the very same grammar the content uses ('Ran the tests', 'Corri os testes'). ANY ordinary noun phrase or declarative sentence in the title means the title MAY carry the fact, and the candidate escapes structurally, with the lexicon never consulted. NOTE THE POLARITY, which is what makes a closed placeholder list safe HERE and made every earlier list a bug: PLACEHOLDER_TITLES is a WHITELIST OF WHAT PERMITS THE VETO. An unlisted title makes the rule REFUSE to fire — an escape, one judge call — never a veto. Under-listing cannot destroy knowledge; it can only cost a judge call. THE VETO REMAINS ALIVE for real chatter, whose titles are generic or are themselves narration, and the `chatter-title-*` probes below pin exactly that with REAL titles — every pre-v6 chatter probe hardcodes 'Note'/'Nota', so it exercises no title at all and could neither have caught this defect nor prove the fix leaves the veto with anything to catch.

fixtures.retention_reason: Explains the durable architecture of event publishing; a future agent that adds an event would otherwise publish inside the transaction and lose events on rollback.

candidate.title: Domain events are published through an outbox table, never inline
candidate.content: We publish domain events by writing them to the `event_outbox` table inside the same transaction as the aggregate write, and a relay worker ships them to the broker afterwards. Publishing inline from the service was dropped because a broker timeout rolled back an order that had already been charged. Any new event must go through OutboxWriter::append(); direct calls to the broker client from application code are rejected in review.

fixtures.retention_reason: Records why reads bypass the ORM; without it someone will 'simplify' the reporting endpoints back into eager-loaded relations and reintroduce the N+1 blowup.

candidate.title: Reporting endpoints read from projections, not from the write model
candidate.content: Reporting queries are served from the `report_daily_totals` projection, which the nightly job rebuilds. The write-model aggregates are not eager-loaded for reports because a single dashboard request previously issued more than 400 queries. The rule is: no reporting controller may touch an Eloquent aggregate root directly; it must query a projection table.

fixtures.retention_reason: Single-writer invariant is invisible in the code; an agent adding a second write path would silently break idempotency.

candidate.title: KnowledgeWriter is the single write path for knowledge entries
candidate.content: Every ingestion source (condense, MCP, CLI, import) goes through KnowledgeWriter. We centralized this after two sources drifted apart and one of them stopped attaching tags. Because the writer owns the transaction boundary, any post-commit side effect must be registered there and never scheduled from a controller.

fixtures.retention_reason: Explains why a dedicated queue exists; merging it back into the default queue would starve interactive jobs behind slow model calls.

candidate.title: Model-backed jobs run on their own queue
candidate.content: Jobs that call an external model run on the `classification` queue, isolated from `default`. They were moved off the default queue because a 90-second model timeout blocked notification delivery for every other job on the worker. New model-backed jobs must declare their queue explicitly; relying on the default connection is a review failure.

fixtures.retention_reason: Hard business rule with a boundary condition that is easy to get wrong and is not written anywhere else.

candidate.title: Refunds are allowed within 14 days of delivery, not of purchase
candidate.content: A refund request is accepted when it arrives within 14 days of the delivery timestamp. Purchase date is irrelevant, because slow shipments used to consume the whole window before the customer even received the parcel. Orders without a delivery timestamp are never auto-refundable and must be routed to manual review.

fixtures.retention_reason: Legal numbering constraint; violating it produces invalid invoices and is not enforced by the schema.

candidate.title: Invoice numbers are gapless per fiscal year and never reused
candidate.content: Invoice numbers must be sequential and gapless within a fiscal year. A cancelled invoice keeps its number and is offset by a credit note; the number is never recycled, because tax audits treat a missing number as a deleted invoice. The sequence is allocated by `InvoiceNumberAllocator` under a row lock, so no code may compute the next number with max(id) + 1.

fixtures.retention_reason: Non-obvious lifecycle rule with a data-retention consequence; a wrong implementation deletes customer data.

candidate.title: An expired trial downgrades to the free plan and never deletes data
candidate.content: When a trial expires the account moves to the `free` plan with read-only access to anything above the free quota. Data is never deleted on downgrade, because a customer who upgrades later expects their projects back. Deletion only happens after an explicit account-closure request plus the 30-day grace period.

fixtures.retention_reason: Tax rule with an exact condition set; getting it wrong produces incorrect invoices in a whole market.

candidate.title: Reverse-charge VAT applies only to validated EU business customers outside the seller country
candidate.content: We must apply reverse charge only when the customer is a business, holds a VAT number validated by VIES, and is established in an EU country other than the seller's. If VIES validation is unavailable we charge domestic VAT instead of assuming reverse charge, because an unverified exemption is our liability, not the customer's.

fixtures.retention_reason: Operational constraint tied to a database column type; changing the model without this knowledge corrupts the vector index.

candidate.title: The embedding dimension is locked to 768 by the vector column
candidate.content: `chunk_embeddings.embedding` is declared as vector(768), so the embedding model must produce 768 dimensions. Changing RAG_EMBEDDING_DIM alone will fail at insert time and leaves the index half-populated. Switching models requires a migration of the column plus a full re-embed of every chunk, in that order.

fixtures.retention_reason: Prevents a production outage; the reason (ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock queueing) is invisible from the migration file.

candidate.title: Production migrations must set a lock_timeout before altering hot tables
candidate.content: Any ALTER TABLE on `orders` or `sessions` must run with `SET lock_timeout = '3s'`. Without it, the migration waits for an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock and every subsequent query queues behind it, which is how the checkout outage happened. If the migration times out, retry it during the low-traffic window rather than raising the timeout.

fixtures.retention_reason: Encodes an external quota and the concrete backoff that keeps us inside it; rediscovering it costs a day of throttled traffic.

candidate.title: The shipping provider allows 60 requests per minute per account
candidate.content: The shipping API rejects everything above 60 requests per minute per account with HTTP 429 and a 10-minute cooldown penalty. The sync worker must therefore stay at a maximum concurrency of 4 with a 1-second delay between batches. Never raise concurrency to speed up a backlog: the cooldown makes the backlog worse, not better.

fixtures.retention_reason: Non-obvious deployment constraint linking worker memory to an OOM killer; without it the next tuning attempt reintroduces silent job loss.

candidate.title: Queue workers run with --memory=256 because the container limit is 512Mi
candidate.content: Workers are started with `--memory=256` so PHP restarts the worker gracefully before the container's 512Mi limit triggers the OOM killer. An OOM-killed worker loses the job it was reserving, and the job is only recovered after the visibility timeout. Raising the worker memory flag requires raising the container limit in the same change.

fixtures.retention_reason: Convention with a hard failure mode (config:cache) that an agent will otherwise break on the first custom resolver.

candidate.title: Never put closures in config files
candidate.content: Config values must be var_export-safe: use a class-method array such as [MyResolver::class, 'resolve'] instead of a closure. A closure in any config file breaks `php artisan config:cache`, and setting it from a service provider does not help because config:cache boots providers before it snapshots the config.

fixtures.retention_reason: Project-wide convention that keeps DB check constraints and PHP in sync; a hand-typed option list silently drifts.

candidate.title: Finite value sets are PHP enums, and the enum is the only source of options
candidate.content: Every finite value set is a backed PHP enum, and select options must come from `SomeEnum::values()` rather than a hand-written array. We adopted this after a status option list drifted from the database check constraint and produced rows the application refused to read back. A new value must be added to the enum and to the check constraint in the same migration.

fixtures.retention_reason: Convention that prevents flaky tests and accidental network calls; the reason is not discoverable from the test file.

candidate.title: The test suite fakes the queue globally so no test reaches the embedder
candidate.content: The base TestCase calls Queue::fake() in setUp, because under the sync queue connection creating a knowledge entry would dispatch the index job and hit the real embedder on localhost. A test that needs to assert real indexing must dispatch the job explicitly instead of removing the global fake.

fixtures.retention_reason: Convention with an exact key-parity requirement that is enforced nowhere in code.

candidate.title: Every user-facing string ships in en, pt_PT and pt_BR with identical keys
candidate.content: User-visible copy must go through __() and exist in all three locale files with the same key set. A key present in one locale only renders as the raw key in the others, which is how the billing modal shipped showing `edgeflow.gates.pro.title` to Brazilian users. Placeholders such as :plan must be passed as arguments and never concatenated.

fixtures.retention_reason: A fix whose cause (implicit cast disabling an index) is genuinely non-obvious and will be reintroduced by anyone writing a raw query.

candidate.title: Comparing a uuid column to a string literal disables the index
candidate.content: The slow lookup on `sessions.uuid` was caused by comparing the uuid column with a text parameter, which forces a cast and skips the btree index. Binding the parameter as uuid (or casting explicitly with ::uuid) restored the index scan and cut the query from 900ms to 3ms. Any raw query against a uuid column must bind the parameter with the correct type.

fixtures.retention_reason: Subtle data bug with a precise root cause; the wrong fix (shifting the value) hides it instead of solving it.

candidate.title: Daily aggregates were off by one day because timestamps were converted twice
candidate.content: The daily totals job grouped by a timestamp that had already been converted to the application timezone, and then converted it again on render, which shifted every row before 01:00 into the previous day. The fix is to keep all stored timestamps in UTC and convert only at the presentation boundary. Never call setTimezone() inside a query builder expression.

fixtures.retention_reason: A correctness rule about error handling that a broad catch block will violate again; the failure it prevents is silent data loss.

candidate.title: Catch SQLSTATE 23505 specifically, not every QueryException
candidate.content: The idempotent upsert used to catch QueryException wholesale and treat it as a lost race, which swallowed a not-null violation and left the row unwritten for hours. Only SQLSTATE 23505 means a unique-key race; every other QueryException must be rethrown so it surfaces as a real failure.

fixtures.retention_reason: Records a rejected alternative and why; without it the vendor SDK gets reintroduced on the next integration.

candidate.title: We call the payment provider over plain HTTP instead of its official SDK
candidate.content: We decided to talk to the payment provider through the framework HTTP client rather than the vendor SDK. The SDK pins an old guzzle constraint and swallows the idempotency key on retries, which produced a double charge in staging. The cost is that we maintain our own request signing, which lives in PaymentSigner and is covered by contract tests.

fixtures.retention_reason: Explicitly rejected approach with its rationale; the alternative looks attractive and would be re-proposed otherwise.

candidate.title: Rejected knowledge entries are kept as rejected instead of being deleted
candidate.content: We chose to keep rejected entries in the table with status `rejected` rather than deleting them, because a rejected entry is our only evidence when we tune the classifier and want to measure false rejections. Rejected entries are excluded from search and from the approval queue, so they cost visibility nothing.

fixtures.retention_reason: Rollout decision with exit criteria; skipping shadow mode would silently reject real knowledge.

candidate.title: The importance classifier ships in shadow mode before it may reject anything
candidate.content: The classifier is deployed with mode `shadow` first: it records a verdict but never rejects an entry. We will only switch to `enforce` after at least 50 reviewed entries show zero false rejections on the must-keep corpus, because an automatic rejection is invisible to the person who wrote the knowledge. Rolling back is a mode change, not a deployment.

fixtures.retention_reason: Interface contract with a latency rationale; a synchronous implementation would time out the MCP client.

candidate.title: The MCP store tool answers immediately and classifies asynchronously
candidate.content: rag_store_knowledge must return as soon as the entry is persisted and report that classification was scheduled. It must not wait for the model call, because a 90-second judge call exceeds the MCP client timeout and the agent retries the whole tool call, creating duplicates. The entry is created as `classifying` and the job transitions it afterwards.

fixtures.retention_reason: Convention whose violation is silently erased by composer install; the reason must be stated once and remembered.

candidate.title: Never edit files under vendor/
candidate.content: Changes under `vendor/` are erased by the next composer install, so a fix applied there is lost and the bug returns in production while it stays fixed on the developer machine. Extend the package through its documented hooks instead, and if no hook exists, open an upstream issue and keep a local override in the host application.

fixtures.retention_reason: Adversarial regression for the agent_operation_only veto: the sentence uses 'exit code', vocabulary the marker list used to key on, but it states an ordinary technical fact, not agent-operation narration.

candidate.title: A non-zero embedder exit code means the model file is missing
candidate.content: A non-zero exit code from the embedder means the model file is missing, so the job must not retry until an operator restores the file: retrying only reproduces the same failure forever.

fixtures.retention_reason: Adversarial regression for the agent_operation_only veto: the sentence names 'tool call' as a fact about an API contract, not as narration of the agent's own actions.

candidate.title: Every tool call to rag_store_knowledge must include a project_id
candidate.content: Every tool call to `rag_store_knowledge` must include a project_id, because omitting it silently files the entry under the wrong project and the mistake stays invisible until someone searches for it later.

fixtures.retention_reason: Adversarial regression for the agent_operation_only veto: the sentence reports a durable CI policy that happens to use 'all tests pass', not an agent narrating a test run it just performed.

candidate.title: Branch protection blocks merges unless all tests pass
candidate.content: The branch protection rule blocks merging unless all tests pass on the `main` pipeline, because a red pipeline previously reached production and took checkout down for six minutes. Never bypass the check with an admin merge.

fixtures.retention_reason: Adversarial regression for the empty_content veto: an 18-character rule is short but fully readable, not empty or unreadable, and must never be stamped with the empty_content audit reason.

candidate.title: Never edit vendor code
candidate.content: Never edit vendor.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'task completed' inside a declarative sentence about the worker. v1 vetoed it as agent narration.

candidate.title: Worker runs are recorded in job_runs
candidate.content: Every task completed by the worker writes a row to job_runs.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Same marker in a passive-voice scheduling rule. v1 vetoed it.

candidate.title: Late tasks are discarded
candidate.content: A task completed after its deadline is discarded by the scheduler queue.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'let us' in its 'allowed us' sense, mid-sentence. v1 vetoed it.

candidate.title: pgvector replaced Pinecone
candidate.content: Moving to pgvector let us drop the Pinecone dependency from the stack.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Portuguese: 'a executar' as an ordinary progressive verb about the worker, not first-person narration. v1 vetoed it.

candidate.title: Lotes do worker de embeddings
candidate.content: O worker de embeddings está a executar em lotes de 32 vetores.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'here is a summary' is a legitimate condensation opener over durable content. v1 vetoed it.

candidate.title: Retry policy of the job runner
candidate.content: Here is a summary of the retry policy: 3 attempts, then the job is parked.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Sentence-initial gerund over the agent's own tooling, but the gerund is the SUBJECT of a declarative sentence, not a progress report.

candidate.title: Parallel test runs leak shared memory
candidate.content: Running the test suite twice in the same container leaks 400 MB of shared memory.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Same shape, phrased around a test outcome that a reviewer would expect the veto to over-trigger on.

candidate.title: Parallel tests corrupt the sqlite database
candidate.content: Running the tests in parallel corrupts the shared sqlite database.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'reading the file' opener whose tail is a declarative comparison, not a progress tail.

candidate.title: fgets() is slower per chunk
candidate.content: Reading the file through fgets() adds 40 ms per chunk compared with file_get_contents().

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'all tests pass' as a clause inside a longer CI statement, not a terse status report.

candidate.title: Release branch needs the seeder first
candidate.content: All tests pass on the release branch only after the seeder has populated the demo tenant.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'we run' in the present tense is the canonical voice of a convention, not agent narration.

candidate.title: Model-backed jobs run on their own queue
candidate.content: We run model-backed jobs on the classification queue, away from the default queue.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'we read' with a system object; the veto must only key on first-person narration of a tool run.

candidate.title: Shipping quota per account
candidate.content: We read the shipping quota from the provider dashboard: 60 requests per minute per account.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Passive voice over the exact vocabulary the veto keys on (test suite, run, CI).

candidate.title: CI runs the suite on every push
candidate.content: The test suite is run in CI on every push, and a red run blocks the merge.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Error-code knowledge with no positive marker at all.

candidate.title: Exit code 137 is an OOM kill
candidate.content: Exit code 137 marks an OOM kill by the container runtime.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Deploy knowledge in the passive voice, no positive marker.

candidate.title: Deploys queue behind the migration lock
candidate.content: Deploys to production are queued while a migration holds the advisory lock.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Portuguese: 'tarefa concluída' mid-sentence in a passive scheduling rule.

candidate.title: Tarefas fora do prazo
candidate.content: A tarefa concluída fora do prazo é descartada pela fila do scheduler.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Portuguese: third-person 'corre os testes' describing the deploy command, not the agent.

candidate.title: O deploy corre os testes
candidate.content: O comando de deploy corre os testes antes de publicar a imagem.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'I ran into' is a phrasal verb, not a tool run; the first-person anchor must not fire on it.

candidate.title: Concurrent updates deadlock on the same row
candidate.content: I ran into a deadlock when two workers updated the same row.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: First-person, but it asserts a durable fact about the schema; a mutation of a SYSTEM thing must escape to the judge, not be vetoed.

candidate.title: Unique index on chunks.hash
candidate.content: I added a unique index on chunks.hash after the duplicate rows appeared.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'done' as the subject of a definition, not a terse status report.

candidate.title: What done means for a job row
candidate.content: Done means the job row carries a finished_at timestamp.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'Let's Encrypt' is a certificate authority, not an agent saying "let's". Found by adversarial probing: a bare let-opener false-vetoed it, so the let-form now requires a following operational verb.

candidate.title: Certificate renewal window
candidate.content: Let's Encrypt certificates renew 30 days before expiry.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'checking the file' opener whose tail is a declarative clause about the importer.

candidate.title: The importer compares content hashes
candidate.content: Checking the file mtime is not enough; the importer compares content hashes.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: 'the tool call' as an API contract fact; v1 keyed on this phrase.

candidate.title: Tool call response envelope
candidate.content: The tool call returns a JSON envelope with a content array.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: v2's progress tail treated ANY comma-introduced tail as a progress report, so this CI timing fact was hard-vetoed. v3 has no gerund branch at all.

candidate.title: The suite takes 12 minutes on CI
candidate.content: Running the tests, even with --parallel, takes 12 minutes on CI.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: same comma-tail branch, chunked-read fact.

candidate.title: Memory spike on large imports
candidate.content: Reading the file, chunk by chunk, avoids the memory spike on large imports.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: same comma-tail branch, container limit fact.

candidate.title: Docker tmpfs limit during tests
candidate.content: Running the tests, in the Docker image, hits the 2 GB tmpfs limit.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: v2's tail branch also swallowed a colon-introduced declarative clause.

candidate.title: The audit job compares content hashes
candidate.content: Checking the file: the audit job compares content hashes, not mtime.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: v2 matched the adverb 'first' wherever it landed and let '.*' swallow the declarative predicate.

candidate.title: Hash check before the importer runs
candidate.content: Checking the file first avoids a race with the importer.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: same adverb branch, 'then' as a coordinator rather than a progress adverb.

candidate.title: Deploying without a rebuild ships stale assets
candidate.content: Running the tests then deploying without a rebuild ships stale assets.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: same adverb branch, 'next to' as a preposition.

candidate.title: Tests deadlock a concurrent migration
candidate.content: Running the tests next to a migration deadlocks the orders table.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: v2's first-person INSPECTION branch had no object constraint, so a first-person report about a SYSTEM thing was vetoed. v3 requires a tooling-artefact object on every first-person branch.

candidate.title: ivfflat caps at 2000 dimensions
candidate.content: I read the pgvector source and the ivfflat index caps at 2000 dimensions.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: same missing object constraint; 'the container' is a system thing, not a tooling artefact.

candidate.title: OOM killer threshold
candidate.content: I inspected the container and the OOM killer fires at 512 MB.

must_not_be_vetoed.why: Round-3 false veto: the object IS a tooling artefact ('the tests'), so only the knowledge-assertion safety net can save it — this probe pins that net.

candidate.title: jsonb ordering changed in Postgres 16

must_not_be_vetoed.why: The direction of failure under the title whitelist, pinned. A title in a shape or a language nobody enumerated is neither a listed placeholder nor recognised narration, so the veto REFUSES to fire and the candidate escapes — one judge call.
